message:
harden utime_access_openat_statvfs probe and fix statfs/statvfs scenario tag (bd-n8blq)

Two small follow-ups on top of commit 47b5bf6
("extend ext4 syscall-level differential conformance with utime,
access, openat dir_fd, and statvfs probes").

`crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs`:

- Tolerate Python runtimes that lack `os.supports_dir_fd`. The new
  step previously did `if os.open in os.supports_dir_fd: ...`, which
  raises `AttributeError` on Python 3.2 and any stripped-down embed
  without the constant. Replace with:

      supports_dir_fd = getattr(os, "supports_dir_fd", set())
      if os.open in supports_dir_fd:
          ...

  An empty `set()` makes the membership check return False without
  raising, so the probe falls through to the existing
  `python_dir_fd_unavailable` sentinel branch instead of aborting
  the differential script.

- Fix the `SCENARIO_RESULT` detail string the test emits when the
  ext4 syscall-level differential conformance run completes. The
  previous commit wrote `...+access+statfs+dir_ops+...`, which is a
  typo: the harness probes `statvfs(2)`, not `statfs(2)`. Switch to
  `...+access+statvfs+dir_ops+...` so downstream coverage parsers
  see the correct probe family name.

`.beads/issues.jsonl`:

- Update `bd-n8blq`'s notes to mention the dir_fd capability hardening
  and the scenario-tag fix on top of the earlier 47b5bf6 metadata
  entry, and bump `updated_at` to 2026-04-26T16:04:22.209096193Z.
  Status stays `in_progress` (cod-fs assignee).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
